# ATECO (Classification of Economic Activity) 2007

Since January 2008 Istat has adopted the new Ateco 2007 classification of economic activities. The migration of economic statistics to the new classification follows a specific calendar for individual statistical surveys and the same for all EU countries.

This classification is the national version of the European nomenclature, Nace Rev. 2, published in the Official Journal of 20 December 2006 (Regulation (EC) no 1893/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 20 December 2006).

Ateco 2007 has been set out and approved by a specially convened Steering Committee. In addition to Istat, which coordinates it, it requires the participation of several institutional figures: the Ministries concerned, the Bodies which manage the main administrative data sources on enterprises (the Revenue Agency, Chambers of Commerce, social security institutions, etc.) and the main business associations.

By working in close collaboration with the Revenue Agency and Chambers of Commerce a single classification has been made. For the first time the world of official statistics, the Revenue Agency and Chambers of Commerce adopted the same classification of economic activities. This result represents a significant step forward in integrating and simplifying the information gathered and handled by Public Administration.

An automatic coding tool makes possible to assign an Ateco 2007 code according to a brief description of the economic activity supplied by the users. It is available also a tool for browsing the whole text of the classification and for identifying parts of the classification in which a specific word has been entered by the user.

All documents of Istat’s Ateco 2007 and the tool are only [available in Italian language](https://www.istat.it/it/archivio/17888).

For more in depth information, please refer to [Migration of short-term statistics to the Ateco 2007](https://www.istat.it/en/archive/17862) or send an email to [ateco2007@istat.it](mailto:ateco2007@istat.it)

web.title: ATECO Italy: Classificazione Delle Attività Aconomiche
web.description: The Classificazione Delle Attività Aconomiche (ATECO) is the official framework for classifying business establishments in Italy.
web.markdown:
The Classificazione Delle Attività Aconomiche (ATECO) is the official framework for classifying business establishments in Italy. ATECO, produced and maintained by the Istituto Nazionale di Statistica (ISTAT), was first published in 1981 and has been revised six times, most recently at the end of 2024. ATECO, which is based on the European Union's NACE, is structured into six levels, from broad sections at the highest level to more specific sub-categories at the bottom reflecting Italy's industrial structure. ATECO is used widely across public agencies and private organizations in Italy.

## Metadata

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Publisher type | National |
| Publisher(s) | - [Istituto Nazionale di Statistica](https://www.istat.it/en) |
| Jurisdictions | Italy |

### Available versions

1. [ATECO 2025 _(Current)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_1_ateco-2025)
2. [ATECO 2007 v. 2022 _(Legacy)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_2_ateco-2007-v-2022)
3. [ATECO 2007 v. 2021 _(Legacy)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_3_ateco-2007-v-2021)
4. [ATECO 2007 _(Legacy)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_4_ateco-2007)
5. [ATECO 2002 _(Legacy)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_5_ateco-2002)
6. [ATECO 1991 _(Legacy)_](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/ateco#version_6_ateco-1991)

## Versions

## ATECO 2025 _(Current)_

ATECO 2025 took effect on 1 January 2025. It is identical to the [European Union's NACE Rev. 2.1](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/nace) up to the four-digit (Class) level.

#### Changes Between ATECO 2007 v. 2022 and ATECO 2025

ATECO 2007 v. 2022 has been comprehensively revised in ATECO 2025. Codes have been introduced, deleted, and retitled across all sections of the system. The changes introduced in the classification levels 1-4 of ATECO 2025 reflect those introduced in the newly released NACE Rev. 2.1 with the number of industries increasing in all but the two-digit (Division) level. The more detailed levels (5 and 6) were also expanded with several industries established for emerging activities like _influencers_ with the sub-category 73.11.03 ( _Influencer marketing_). Notable additions include categories covering other professional research activities such as 72.10.1 ( _Research and experimental development on biotechnology_) and 70.10.2 ( _Other research and experimental development on natural sciences and engineering_) which has the new subcategories 70.10.21 ( _Research and experimental development on geology_) and 70.10.22 ( _Research and experimental development on conservation science_).

#### Structure of ATECO 2025

ATECO 2025 is structured into six levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 22 Sections identified by the letters A to V.

- **Division**: 87 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes (01-99).

- **Group**: 287 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes (01.1-99.0).

- **Class**: 651 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes (01.11-99.00).

- **Categories**: 920 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes (01.11.0-99.00.0).

- **Sub-categories**: 1290 Sub-categories identified by six-digit numeric codes (01.11.00-99.00.00).


The table shows the composition of each of the 22 sections of ATECO 2025. At the five and six-digit level, the Section C ( _Manufacturing_) is the most detailed section with 308 Categories and 403 Sub-categories followed by Section G ( _Wholesale and retail trade_) with 174 Categories and 270 Sub-categories. In having two additional levels, Italy's ATECO approach to adapting the NACE system differs from other national classification systems in Europe which generally add one additional level, see e.g. [Germany's WZ](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/wz), [the Netherland's SBI](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/sbi), and [France's NAF](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/naf). As an example, under the NACE Rev. 2.1 Class 31.00 ( _Manufacture of furniture_), ATECO 2025 has added the five-digit Categories 31.00.1 ( _Manufacture of furniture for shops, offices and other similar places_), 31.00.2 ( _Manufacture of kitchen furniture_), and 31.00.3 ( _Manufacture of other home furniture_). The Category 31.00.3 has eight Sub-categories including 31.00.31 ( _Manufacture of home furniture, excluding kitchen furniture, chairs, sofas and similar products_), 31.00.32 ( _Manufacture of outdoor furniture_), and 31.00.33 ( _Manufacture of chairs and seats_).

| Section ID | Section Title | Divisions | Groups | Classes | Categories | Sub-categories |

| A | Agriculture, fishing and forestry | 3 | 14 | 39 | 48 | 57 |
| B | Mining and quarrying | 5 | 10 | 15 | 15 | 18 |

#### Resources

- [ATECO 2025 (Current) - Codes with titles -Excel](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/StrutturaATECO-2025-IT-EN-1.xlsx)

- [ATECO 2025 (Current) - Codes with titles](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Nota-metodologica-1.pdf)


## ATECO 2007 v. 2022 _(Legacy)_

ATECO 2007 v. 2022 was in force from 1 January 2022 to 31 December 2024. It is identical to the [European Union's NACE Rev. 2](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/nace) up to the four-digit (Class) level.

#### Changes Between ATECO 2007 v. 2021 and ATECO 2007 v. 2022

In ATECO 2007 v. 2022 the contents and titles of codes were updated, and new categories and sub-categories were created. A major reorganization was done in Section M ( _Professional, scientific, and technical activities_) where the category 74.90.1 ( _Agricultural consultancy_) was given more detail with the new sub-categories 74.90.13 ( _Agricultural consultancy provided by agricultural experts_) and 74.90.14 ( _Agricultural consultancy provided by other economists specializing in agriculture_) both derived from the ATECO 2007 v. 2021 sub-category 74.90.12 ( _Agricultural consultancy provided by agricultural technicians and agricultural experts_). Secondly, category 74.90.3 ( _Environmental and energy saving consultancy_) was created with three subcategories 74.90.31-74.31.33 covering different aspects environmental and energy consulting. In summary, between both versions the number of five-digit categories increased from 618 to 620 and the number of sub-categories increased from 1226 to 1241.

#### Structure of ATECO 2007 v. 2022

ATECO 2007 v. 2022 is structured into five levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 21 Sections identified by the letters A to U.

- **Division**: 88 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes (01-99).

- **Group**: 271 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes (01.1-99.0).

- **Class**: 615 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes (01.11-99.00).

- **Categories**: 920 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes (01.11.1-99.00.0).

- **Sub-categories**: 1241 Sub-categories identified by six-digit numeric codes (01.11.10-99.00.00).


#### Resources

- [ATECO 2007 v. 2022 (Current) - Codes with titles (in Italian) - Excel](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Struttura-ATECO-2007-aggiornamento-2022.xlsx)

- [ATECO 2007 v. 2022 - Summary of updates in 2022 (in Italian)](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/Allegato-Ateco2007-aggiornamento2022-Struttura.pdf)


## ATECO 2007 v. 2021 _(Legacy)_

ATECO 2007 v. 2021 was in force from 1 January 2001 to 31 December 2021. It is identical to the [European Union's NACE Rev. 2](https://classification.codes/classifications/industry/nace) up to the four-digit (Class) level.

#### Changes Between ATECO 2007 and ATECO 2007 v. 2021

2007 v. 2021 was a minor update of ATECO 2007 affecting only the agricultural sector where four sub-categories were created including 01.19.21 ( _Growing of flowers in protected out-of-soil crops_) and 01.19.29 ( _Growing of flowers in protected out-of-soil crops excluding out-of-soil crops_).

#### Structure of ATECO 2007 v. 2021

ATECO 2007 v. 2021 is structured into five levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 21 Sections identified by the letters A to U.

- **Division**: 88 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes (01-99).

- **Group**: 272 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes (01.1-99.0).

- **Class**: 615 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes (01.11-99.00).

- **Categories**: 918 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes (01.11.1-99.00.0).

- **Sub-categories**: 1226 Sub-categories identified by six-digit numeric codes (01.11.10-99.00.00).


#### Resources

- [ATECO 2007 v. 2021 - Codes with titles (in Italian) - Excel](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Sintesi-aggiornamenti-2007-vs-2021.xlsx)

- [ATECO 2007 v. 2021 - Summary of updates in 2021 - (in Italian)](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Classificazione_attivita_economicheAteco2007_agg2021.pdf)


## ATECO 2007 _(Legacy)_

ATECO 2007 was in force from 1 January 2008 to 31 December 2024, during which it was revised in 2021 and 2022. It corresponds to NACE Rev. 2 up to the four-digit (Class) level.

#### Changes Between ATECO 2002 and ATECO 2007

ATECO 2002 was comprehensively reviewed in ATECO 2007. From the first to the fourth level of the system, the changes reflect the move from NACE Rev. 1.1 to NACE Rev. 2. In terms of structure, the number of classification levels increased from five in ATECO 2002 to six in ATECO 2007 with the addition of six-digit _sub-categories_. Consequently, several five-digit categories were given further detail e.g. the category 86.22.0 ( _Specialist medical practices and outpatient clinics_) which has the sub-categories 86.22.01 ( _Health services provided by surgeons_), 86.22.02 ( _Outpatient clinics and outpatient clinics of the National Health Service_), 86.22.03 ( _Activities of radiotherapy centers_), 86.22.04 ( _Activities of dialysis centers_), 86.22.05 ( _Homeopathy and acupuncture practices_), 86.22.06 ( _Aesthetic medicine centers_), and 86.22.09 ( _Other specialist medical practices and outpatient clinics_). In summary, the number of five-digit categories increased from 883 in ATECO 2002 to 918 in ATECO 2007 under which were 1224 newly established six-digit sub-categories.

#### Structure of ATECO 2007

ATECO 2007 is structured into six levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 21 Sections identified by the letters A to U.

- **Division**: 88 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes (01-99).

- **Group**: 272 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes (01.1-99.0).

- **Class**: 615 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes (01.11-99.00).

- **Categories**: 918 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes (01.11.1-99.00.0).

- **Sub-categories**: 1224 Sub-categories identified by six-digit numeric codes (01.11.10-99.00.00).


#### Resources

- [ATECO 2007 - Codes with titles (in Italian) - Excel](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Struttura-ATECO-2007.xlsx)

- [ATECO 2007 - Manual (in Italian)](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/volume_integrale_ATECO2007.pdf)


## ATECO 2002 _(Legacy)_

ATECO 2002 was in force from 2003 to 2007. It is identical to the European Union's NACE Rev. 1.1 up to the four-digit (Class) level.

#### Changes Between ATECO 1991 and ATECO 2002

From the first to the fourth level ATECO 2002, the changes reflect the move from NACE Rev. 1 to NACE Rev. 1.1 which was a relatively minor update. At the detailed level, five-digit categories were created and deleted across the system. Most of the new five-digit categories in ATECO 2002 were in the Section I ( _Transport, storage and communications_), Section J ( _Financial activities_), and Section O ( _Other public, social and personal services_).

#### Structure of ATECO 2002

ATECO 2002 is structured into five levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 17 Sections identified by the letters A to Q. Two sections (C and D) are further divided into a total of 16 sub-sections identified by two letters (e.g. CA and CB).

- **Division**: 62 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes (01-99).

- **Group**: 224 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes (01.1-99.0).

- **Class**: 514 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes (01.11-99.00).

- **Categories**: 883 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes (01.11.1-99.00.0).


#### Resources

- [ATECO 2002 - Manual](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Volume-integrale-ATECO-2002.pdf)


## ATECO 1991 _(Legacy)_

ATECO 1991 was in force from 1991 to 2002. It is identical to the European Union's NACE Rev. 1 up to the four-digit (Class) level.

### Structure of ATECO 1991

ATECO 1991 is structured into five levels, as follows:

- **Section**: 17 Sections identified by the letters A to Q. Two sections (C and D) are further divided into a total of 16 sub-sections identified by two letters (e.g. CA and CB).

- **Division**: 60 Divisions identified by two-digit numeric codes.

- **Group**: 222 Groups identified by three-digit numeric codes.

- **Class**: 512 Classes identified by four-digit numeric codes.

- **Categories**: 874 Categories identified by five-digit numeric codes.


### Versions prior to ATECO 1991

[ATECO 1981 was published in 1981](https://www.istat.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Volume-integrale-ATECO-2002.pdf) which was partially based on the general nomenclature of economic activities in the European Community, which was not subject to Community regulation.

#### Resources

- [ATECO 1991 - Manual (in Italian)](http://www.tno.it/patti/ateco91.htm)


## Where is it used?

- Istat is the main user of ATECO codes in its statistical publications such as the [industrial production index](https://www.istat.it/it/files//2024/01/INDUSTRIAL-PRODUCTION-INDEX-english-version.pdf). ATECO codes are included in [business registers and other administrative databases](https://www.istat.it/it/files/2016/11/1_luzi.pdf) maintained by Istat.

- ATECO codes are used to define where certain regulations apply. For example, ATECO codes were used to identify [which businesses in Italy were to close during the COVID-19 lockdowns](https://www.gop.it/doc_covid/51.pdf) and [tax rates defined by the Italian Revenue Agency](https://www.consulia.it/fiscal-regime-for-vat-holders-in-italy/?lang=en) differ according to ATECO codes.

- In the private sector, ATECO codes are included in large databases on Italian companies such as the [AIDA database from Bureau van Dijk](https://www.sba.unipi.it/sites/default/files/aida_descrizione_banca_dati.pdf). Such databases are used for different aspects of market research and investment analysis.

- ATECO codes are commonly used in academic research investigating various issues, from the [risk of mafia capture of Italian businesses](https://ifs.org.uk/sites/default/files/2023-10/WP202328-Mafia-infiltrations-in-times-of-crisis-Evidence-from-the-Covid-19-shock-1_0.pdf) to [occupational exposure to nanoparticles](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S095965260700114X#bib17).


## Further resources

### Other resources

- [Istat - Industrial Production Index](https://www.istat.it/it/files//2024/01/INDUSTRIAL-PRODUCTION-INDEX-english-version.pdf)

- [The new statistical register “Frame SBS”: overview and perspectives](https://www.istat.it/it/files/2016/11/1_luzi.pdf)

- [25 March 2020 Law Decree: the updated list of permitted activities](https://www.gop.it/doc_covid/51.pdf)

- [Description of Aida Database (In Italian)](https://www.sba.unipi.it/sites/default/files/aida_descrizione_banca_dati.pdf)

- [Mafia infiltrations in times of crisis: Evidence from the Covid-19 shock](https://ifs.org.uk/sites/default/files/2023-10/WP202328-Mafia-infiltrations-in-times-of-crisis-Evidence-from-the-Covid-19-shock-1_0.pdf)

- [Potential occupational exposure to manufactured nanoparticles in Italy](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S095965260700114X#bib17)
